flagSecurePreserved.evidence: CrashReportActivity.onCreate calls window.setFlags(FLAG_SECURE, FLAG_SECURE) at line 84, then enableEdgeToEdge() at line 85. androidx.activity.enableEdgeToEdge (activity-compose 1.10.1) sets SystemBarStyle via WindowInsetsControllerCompat and calls WindowCompat.setDecorFitsSystemWindows(window, false); none of these paths call Window.setFlags with FLAG_SECURE in the mask, so the secure flag bit is not cleared. Crash screen will continue to be excluded from Recents thumbnails and OEM screen-capture collectors.

noNewPermissions.evidence: AndroidManifest.xml not modified in HEAD. Diff in MainActivity does not touch the existing POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission flow, only inserts enableEdgeToEdge() before it.

noNewLogging.evidence: Diff adds one import + one extension call per file. No Log.*, println, Timber, or PII-emitting statements introduced.

noNewExportedComponents.evidence: AndroidManifest.xml unchanged. No new <activity>, <service>, <receiver>, <provider>, or intent-filter exports.

noVersionGuardManipulation.evidence: enableEdgeToEdge() called unconditionally in both Activities. minSdk=31 and the AndroidX API is available from API 21+, so a Build.VERSION.SDK_INT guard is unnecessary and (correctly) absent. No surface for silent regression on EMUI.

dependencyA05A06.evidence: gradle/libs.versions.toml and app/build.gradle.kts unchanged in HEAD. androidx.activity:activity-compose 1.10.1 already on classpath; enableEdgeToEdge() is the documented public entry point. No new transitive dependency surface.

hardcodedSecrets.evidence: No string literals, API keys, tokens, or credentials in the 4-line code diff.

owaspTop10.A01_BrokenAccessControl: n/a — no auth or authorization surface in diff
owaspTop10.A03_Injection: n/a — no user-input handling, no SQL, no command/URL construction
owaspTop10.A04_InsecureDesign: pass — FLAG_SECURE-before-enableEdgeToEdge ordering preserves secure-by-default invariant for the crash recovery screen
owaspTop10.A05_SecurityMisconfiguration: pass — no manual setDecorFitsSystemWindows override, no statusBarColor/navigationBarColor override that could conflict with FLAG_SECURE; uses documented AndroidX entry point
owaspTop10.A06_VulnerableComponents: pass — no new or upgraded dependency
owaspTop10.A08_SoftwareDataIntegrity: n/a — no deserialization, no CI/CD changes

findings.owasp_category: A04: Insecure Design (defense-in-depth observation, not a defect)
findings.title: Consider an instrumentation-test guard for FLAG_SECURE on CrashReportActivity
findings.description: The current ordering (window.setFlags(FLAG_SECURE, FLAG_SECURE) at line 84, then enableEdgeToEdge() at line 85) correctly preserves FLAG_SECURE because androidx.activity 1.10.1's enableEdgeToEdge does not touch the FLAG_SECURE bit. This invariant is contract-stable today but is not asserted by any test. A future AndroidX release that adds e.g. a Window.setFlags call on the secure-flag mask could silently regress the crash-screen privacy guarantee.
findings.impact: If FLAG_SECURE is ever cleared after enableEdgeToEdge, the crash report screen could appear in Recents thumbnails or OEM screen-capture collectors, leaking the crashing-state UI (which may include partial chat content, model name, etc.).
findings.recommendation: Add a one-line androidTest assertion on CrashReportActivity launch: `assertTrue((scenario.activity.window.attributes.flags and WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_SECURE) != 0)`. Treat as a backlog item for the broader Audit Wave (Task 8), not a blocker for Task 4.

notes: Patch is 4 lines of source (+ 2 imports). Outside the scope of the diff was treated as N/A per audit instructions.

FLAG_SECURE behavior verified against the documented AndroidX 1.10.1 contract for enableEdgeToEdge: it manipulates SystemBarStyle and WindowCompat.setDecorFitsSystemWindows only; FLAG_SECURE bit is not in any mask used by that code path.
No hardcoded secrets, no new permissions, no new exported components, no new dependencies, no new logging, no version-guard regression surface.
